content: Create a complete Korean landing page for Archon in the NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage project. The page should clearly introduce Archon as an intelligent coding assistant and project orchestrator, explain its value, highlight key capabilities and workflows, and present a polished modern landing-page experience in Korean. Start from the current codebase, analyze the existing structure, create an implementation plan, design and build the landing page UI, ensure responsive behavior on desktop and mobile, preserve the project's existing patterns where appropriate, validate the result, and prepare it as production-ready work.
archon-create-plan
요청은 구현이 아니라 계획 문서 작성입니다. 먼저 현재 코드베이스 구조와 규칙을 확인하고, 그다음 기존 패턴을 수집해 계획 초안을 만들겠습니다.
루트가 비어 있어서 실제 앱 위치를 다시 찾고 있습니다. `rg`도 설치되어 있지 않아 `find` 기반으로 구조를 탐색하겠습니다.
현재 작업 트리에는 앱 파일이 없고, 같은 프로젝트의 다른 워크트리에 실제 프런트엔드 코드가 있습니다. 이 저장소 구성을 확인해서 계획 대상 소스를 어디서 읽어야 하는지 확정하겠습니다.
`HEAD` 기준 저장소에는 `README.md`만 있고, 다른 워크트리의 앱 파일은 아직 커밋되지 않은 작업물로 보입니다. 계획은 실제 구현 대상 코드 기준으로 잡아야 하므로 원본 워크트리와 해당 파일 상태를 읽겠습니다.
저장소 자체는 최소 상태라서, 기존 패턴을 복제하는 범위가 매우 제한적입니다. 히스토리와 인접 워크트리의 앱 스캐폴드를 참고해 어떤 수준까지 "existing patterns"를 인정할 수 있는지 판단하겠습니다.
이제 코드 패턴 수집을 분리해서 진행합니다. 현재 브랜치엔 앱 코드가 없어서, 같은 프로젝트의 인접 워크트리에 있는 React/Vite 스캐폴드를 기준 패턴으로 조사하되 그 한계를 계획서에 명시하겠습니다.

tool_name: 🔍 Searching: site:react.dev StrictMode reference React 18 official
🔍 Searching: site:vite.dev/guide/assets.html public directory Vite official
🔍 Searching: 'Keeping list items in order with key' in https://react.dev/learn/rendering-lists

content: 패턴과 제약이 충분히 모였습니다. 이제 현재 브랜치가 사실상 빈 저장소라는 점을 전제로, 스캐폴드 생성부터 한국어 랜딩페이지 완성까지 이어지는 실행 계획 문서를 작성하겠습니다.

# 🔍 Comprehensive PR Review

**PR**: #1
**Reviewed by**: 5 specialized agents
**Date**: 2026-04-11T16:06:47Z

---

## Summary

This PR delivers a solid static React + Vite landing page with clear structure, coherent Korean copy, and an aligned README update. The main merge blockers are one configuration issue that can break the advertised TypeScript validation flow and one major coverage gap around the newly added landing-page content. Beyond that, there is one accessibility gap around keyboard focus treatment, one narrower navigation/accessibility test gap, and one low-risk bootstrap error-message improvement. Documentation and comment quality are in good shape for the scope of this change.

**Verdict**: "'`REQUEST_CHANGES`

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| 🔴 CRITICAL | 0 |
| 🟠 HIGH | 2 |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | 2 |
| 🟢 LOW | 1 |

---

## 🔴 Critical Issues (Auto-fixing)

No critical issues were identified.

---

## 🟠 High Issues (Auto-fixing)

### `vite.config.ts` uses a Vitest-only `test` block without Vitest-aware typing
📍 `vite.config.ts:1`

`vite.config.ts` imports `defineConfig` from `vite` while declaring a top-level `test` property. Because `tsconfig.app.json` includes this file in the typed project scope, `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` can fail in the documented Node/npm workflow.

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config";
import react from "@vitejs/plugin-react";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  test: {
    environment: "jsdom",
    globals: true,
    setupFiles: "./src/test/setup.ts",
  },
});
```

</details>

---

### Repeated landing-page content is largely untested
📍 `src/App.tsx:62` / `src/App.test.tsx`

The single `App` test covers only a small slice of the new UI. It does not verify the proof points, capability cards, most workflow content, or the final CTA block, even though those sections hold most of the user-facing value in this PR.

<details>
<summary>View fix</summary>

```typescript
describe("App", () => {
  it("renders the proof points and capability cards", () => {
    render(<App />);

    expect(screen.getByLabelText("핵심 수치")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("1 페이지")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("320px+")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("정적 구성")).toBeInTheDocument();

    expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { level: 3, name: "코드 작성과 리뷰를 한 흐름으로" })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { level: 3, name: "여러 작업을 동시에 조율" })).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { level: 3, name: "검증 가능한 결과물 중심" })).toBeInTheDocument();
  });

  it("renders all workflow steps and the final CTA", () => {
    render(<App />);

    expect(screen.getByText("01")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("02")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByText("03")).toBeInTheDocument();
    expect(screen.getByRole("heading", { level: 2, name: /복잡한 개발 흐름을 정리할 준비가 된 팀이라면/ })).toBeInTheDocument();
  });
});
```

</details>

---

## 🟡 Medium Issues (Needs Decision)

### CTA links remove default browser focus affordance without replacement
📍 `src/styles.css:39`

The CTA links are visually customized, but there is no replacement `:focus-visible` treatment. That weakens keyboard accessibility on the page'"'s primary interactive controls.

**Options**: Fix now | Create issue | Skip

<details>
<summary>View details</summary>

| Option | Approach | Effort | Risk if Skipped |
|--------|----------|--------|-----------------|
| Fix Now | Add explicit "'`:focus-visible` styles for `.primary-cta` and `.secondary-cta` | LOW | Accessibility regression remains in the main interactive controls |
| Create Issue | Defer to separate PR | LOW | Keyboard usability remains degraded until follow-up lands |
| Skip | Accept as-is | NONE | Focus visibility stays weaker than expected accessibility baseline |

**Recommendation**: Fix now, because the change is small and directly improves keyboard usability without materially changing the design.

</details>

---

### Navigation and accessibility contracts are only partially verified
📍 `src/App.tsx:56` / `src/App.test.tsx`

Only one internal anchor is asserted today. The secondary hero CTA, both final CTA links, and the labeled regions used for accessibility navigation are untested.

**Options**: Fix now | Create issue | Skip

<details>
<summary>View details</summary>

| Option | Approach | Effort | Risk if Skipped |
|--------|----------|--------|-----------------|
| Fix Now | Add assertions for all CTA `href` values and the labeled regions | LOW | Internal navigation and accessibility labels may drift silently |
| Create Issue | Defer to separate PR | LOW | Regressions in anchor targets or labels may go undetected |
| Skip | Accept as-is | NONE | User-facing navigation contracts remain only partially covered |

**Recommendation**: Fix now, because these are stable, low-maintenance assertions around key page behavior.

</details>

---

## 🟢 Low Issues

<details>
<summary>View 1 low-priority suggestion</summary>

| Issue | Location | Suggestion |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Bootstrap uses non-null assertion instead of guarded failure | `src/main.tsx:6` | Replace `document.getElementById("root")!` with an explicit guard and descriptive thrown error |

</details>

---

## ✅ What'"'s Good

- The landing page structure is semantic and readable.
- The UI is fully static and does not introduce hidden async failure paths.
- The README matches the repo's runtime requirements and validation commands.
- The existing Vitest + Testing Library setup is a good base for expanding coverage.
- No documentation gaps or comment-accuracy issues were identified in the changed files.

---

## 📋 Suggested Follow-up Issues

- Restore visible keyboard focus styles for landing page CTA links
- Expand landing page navigation and accessibility coverage
- Guard React bootstrap when "'`#root` mount node is missing

---

## Next Steps

1. ⚡ Auto-fix step will address CRITICAL + HIGH issues
2. 📝 Review MEDIUM issues above
3. 🎯 Merge when ready

---

*Reviewed by Archon comprehensive-pr-review workflow*
*Artifacts: `/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/artifacts/runs/b5fc23dc9a7ebf49ee6b5b41f20c5a18/review/`*
EOF

## Workflow Summary

**Plan**: "'`/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/artifacts/runs/b5fc23dc9a7ebf49ee6b5b41f20c5a18/plan.md`

- Added `.gitignore` to keep generated outputs out of the PR.
- Added ESLint/Vitest support and tests to validate the new app beyond the original minimum scaffold.

| Item | Effort | Action |
|------|--------|--------|

| Clarify runtime support in `README.md` | ~5 min | Decide whether Bun is supported or the shim was workflow-only |

**Reply with**: `@archon do quick wins` to auto-fix these.

---

### 📋 Suggested Follow-Up Issues

| Title | Labels |
|-------|--------|

| File | Update |
|------|--------|

**Reply with**: `@archon update docs` to send a docs agent.

---

<details>
<summary>ℹ️ Deferred Items (NOT Building)</summary>

These were **intentionally excluded** from scope:

- No language switcher or locale routing.
- No pricing calculator, signup form backend, or analytics integration.
- No CMS or markdown content pipeline.
- No animation library or video background dependency.
- No user authentication, dashboard, or product application shell.
- No external API calls; all landing-page content remains static.
- No automated unit test suite unless the repo later adds a test runner.
- No full design-system abstraction beyond what the landing page itself needs.

</details>

---

**Artifacts**: `/.archon/workspaces/NewTurn2017/archon-test-landingpage/artifacts/runs/b5fc23dc9a7ebf49ee6b5b41f20c5a18/`

fi'
